title: Inscribed shapes
description: Find missing angles and lengths in inscribed shapes.

assessment_items.question_content:
Angle $C$ is inscribed in circle $O$.  
$\overline{AB}$ is a diameter of circle $O$.

**What is the measure of $\angle A$?**  
[[☃ input-number 1]] $^\circ$



[[☃ image 1]]
hints: Angle $C$ intersects the circle at the endpoints of a diameter.  It intercepts an arc of $180^\circ$.  Because of this, the measure of $\angle C$ is $90^\circ$.

The angles of triangle $ABC$ must add up to $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}
\pink{\text{m}\angle A} + \blue{\text{m}\angle B} + \text{m}\angle C &= 180\\
\pink{\text{m}\angle A} + \blue{60} + 90 &= 180\\
\pink{\text{m}\angle A} + 150&= 180\\
\pink{\text{m}\angle A} &= 30\\
\end{align}$
The measure of $\angle A$ is $30^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle centered at point O. Points A, B, and C all lie on the circle in a clockwise direction. A line segment connects point A to point B as a diameter. A line segment B C is a chord. Line segments O A and O B are radii. Angle C B O is sixty degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
Angle $C$ is inscribed in circle $O$.  
$\overline{AB}$ is a diameter of circle $O$.

**What is the measure of $\angle B$?**  
[[☃ input-number 1]] $^\circ$



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
Angle $C$ intersects the circle at the endpoints of a diameter.  It intercepts an arc of $180^\circ$.  

The measure of an inscribed angle is half the measure of its intercepted arc, so we know that the measure of $\angle C$ is $90^\circ$.

The angles of triangle $ABC$ must add up to $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}
\pink{\text{m}\angle A} + \blue{\text{m}\angle B} + \text{m}\angle C &= 180\\
\pink{46} + \blue{\text{m}\angle B} + 90 &= 180\\
\blue{\text{m}\angle B} + 136 &= 180\\
\blue{\text{m}\angle B} &= 44\\
\end{align}$
The measure of $\angle B$ is $44^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle centered at point O. Points A, B, and C all lie on the circle in a clockwise direction. A line segment connects point A to point B as a diameter. A line segments A C and B C are chords. Line segments O A and O B are radii. Angle C A O is forty-six degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
Angle $C$ is inscribed in circle $O$.  
$\overline{AB}$ is a diameter of circle $O$.

**What is the measure of $\angle A$?**  
[[☃ input-number 1]] $^\circ$



[[☃ image 1]]
hints: Angle $C$ intersects the circle at the endpoints of a diameter.  It intercepts an arc of $180^\circ$.  Because of this, the measure of $\angle C$ is $90^\circ$.

The angles of triangle $ABC$ must add up to $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}
\pink{\text{m}\angle A} + \blue{\text{m}\angle B} + \text{m}\angle C &= 180\\
\pink{\text{m}\angle A} + \blue{37} + 90 &= 180\\
\pink{\text{m}\angle A} + 127 &= 180\\
\pink{\text{m}\angle A} &= 53\\
\end{align}$
The measure of $\angle A$ is $53^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle centered at point O. Points A, B, and C all lie on the circle in a clockwise direction. A line segment connects point A to point B as a diameter. A line segments A C and B C are chords. Line segments O A and O B are radii. Angle C B O is thirty-seven degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
Angle $C$ is inscribed in circle $O$.  
$\overline{AB}$ is a diameter of circle $O$.

**What is the measure of $\angle B$?**  
[[☃ input-number 1]] $^\circ$



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
Angle $C$ intersects the circle at the endpoints of a diameter.  It intercepts an arc of $180^\circ$.  

The measure of an inscribed angle is half the measure of its intercepted arc, so we know that the measure of $\angle C$ is $90^\circ$.

The angles of triangle $ABC$ must add up to $180^\circ$.

$\begin{align}
\pink{\text{m}\angle A} + \blue{\text{m}\angle B} + \text{m}\angle C &= 180\\
\pink{71} + \blue{\text{m}\angle B} + 90 &= 180\\
\blue{\text{m}\angle B} + 161 &= 180\\
\blue{\text{m}\angle B} &= 19\\
\end{align}$
The measure of $\angle B$ is $19^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle centered at point O. Points A, B, and C all lie on the circle in a clockwise direction. Triangle A B C is inscribed in the circle with side A B as a diameter for the circle. Angle B A C is seventy-one degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
Angle $C$ is inscribed in circle $O$.  
$\overline{AB}$ is a diameter of circle $O$.

**What is the radius of circle $O$?**  
[[☃ input-number 1]] units



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
Angle $C$ intersects the circle at the endpoints of a diameter.  It intercepts an arc of $180^\circ$.  

The measure of an inscribed angle is half the measure of its intercepted arc, so we know that the measure of $\angle C$ is $90^\circ$.

Because $\angle C$ is a right angle, we can use the Pythagorean theorem to find the length of $\overline{AB}$.

$\begin{align}
\blue{AC}^2 + \pink{BC}^2 &= AB^2\\
\blue{15}^2 + \pink{8}^2 &= AB^2\\
\blue{225} + \pink{64} &= AB^2 \\
289 &= AB^2 \\
17 &= AB
\end{align}$

The diameter $AB=17$, so the radius of the circle is $8.5$.

*Note: The lengths in this triangle - 8, 15, 17 - are a Pythagorean triple.*

options.alt: A circle centered at point O. Points A, B, and C all lie on the circle in a clockwise direction. Triangle A B C is inscribed in the circle with side A B as a diameter for the circle. Side A C is fifteen units. Side B C is eight units.

assessment_items.question_content:
Angle $C$ is inscribed in circle $O$.  
$\overline{AB}$ is a diameter of circle $O$.

**What is the radius of circle $O$?**  
[[☃ input-number 1]] units



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
Angle $C$ intersects the circle at the endpoints of a diameter.  It intercepts an arc of $180^\circ$.  

The measure of an inscribed angle is half the measure of its intercepted arc, so we know that the measure of $\angle C$ is $90^\circ$.

Because $\angle C$ is a right angle, we can use the Pythagorean theorem to find the length of $\overline{AB}$.

$\begin{align}
\blue{AC}^2 + \pink{BC}^2 &= AB^2\\
\blue{12}^2 + \pink{9}^2 &= AB^2\\
\blue{144} + \pink{81} &= AB^2 \\
225 &= AB^2 \\
15 &= AB
\end{align}$

The diameter $AB=15$, so the radius of the circle is $7.5$.

*Note: The lengths in this triangle - 9, 12, 15 - are a Pythagorean triple.  The values are multiples of the common triple 3, 4, 5.*

options.alt: A circle centered at point O. Points A, B, and C all lie on the circle in a clockwise direction. Triangle A B C is inscribed in the circle with side A B as a diameter for the circle. Side A C is twelve units. Side B C is nine units.

assessment_items.question_content:
Angle $C$ is inscribed in circle $O$.  
$\overline{AB}$ is a diameter of circle $O$.

**What is the radius of circle $O$?**  
[[☃ input-number 1]] units



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
Angle $C$ intersects the circle at the endpoints of a diameter.  It intercepts an arc of $180^\circ$.  

The measure of an inscribed angle is half the measure of its intercepted arc, so we know that the measure of $\angle C$ is $90^\circ$.

Because $\angle C$ is a right angle, we can use the Pythagorean theorem to find the length of $\overline{AB}$.

$\begin{align}
\blue{AC}^2 + \pink{BC}^2 &= AB^2\\
\blue{6}^2 + \pink{8}^2 &= AB^2\\
\blue{36} + \pink{64} &= AB^2 \\
100 &= AB^2 \\
10 &= AB
\end{align}$

The diameter $AB=10$, so the radius of the circle is $5$.

*Note: The lengths in this triangle - 6, 8, 10 - are a Pythagorean triple.  They come from the common Pythagorean triple 3, 4, 5 multiplied by a factor of 2.*

options.alt: A circle centered at point O. Points A, B, and C all lie on the circle in a clockwise direction. Triangle A B C is inscribed in the circle with side A B as a diameter for the circle. Side A C is six units. Side B C is eight units.

assessment_items.question_content:
Angle $C$ is inscribed in circle $O$.  
$\overline{AB}$ is a diameter of circle $O$.

**What is the radius of circle $O$?**  
[[☃ input-number 1]] units



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
Angle $C$ intersects the circle at the endpoints of a diameter.  It intercepts an arc of $180^\circ$.  

The measure of an inscribed angle is half the measure of its intercepted arc, so we know that the measure of $\angle C$ is $90^\circ$.

Because $\angle C$ is a right angle, we can use the Pythagorean theorem to find the length of $\overline{AB}$.

$\begin{align}
\blue{AC}^2 + \pink{BC}^2 &= AB^2\\
\blue{12}^2 + \pink{5}^2 &= AB^2\\
\blue{144} + \pink{25} &= AB^2 \\
169 &= AB^2 \\
13 &= AB
\end{align}$

The diameter $AB=13$, so the radius of the circle is $6.5$.

*Note: The lengths in this triangle - 5, 12, 13 - are a Pythagorean triple.*

options.alt: A circle centered at point O. Points A, B, and C all lie on the circle in a clockwise direction. Triangle A B C is inscribed in the circle with side A B as a diameter for the circle. Side A C is twelve units. Side B C is five units.

assessment_items.question_content:
On circle $O$ below, the measure of $\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{FJ}$ is $84^\circ$.  The measure of $\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{GH}$ is $76^\circ$.

**What is the measure of $\angle HKJ$?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
The angle $\angle FGJ$ is an inscribed angle that intercepts  $\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{FJ}$.

The measure of an inscribed angle is half the measure of the arc that it intercepts.

>$\green{\text{m} \angle FGJ}=\dfrac{1}{2}(84^\circ)=\green{42^\circ}$

By the same reasoning, $\purple{\text{m} \angle GFH}=\dfrac{1}{2}(76^\circ)=\purple{38^\circ}$

>[[☃ image 1]]

Points $G$, $K$, and $F$ form a triangle.

The measures of the interior angles of a triangle add up to $180^\circ$.  

>$\text{m}\angle GKF = 180^\circ - \green{42^\circ} - \purple{38^\circ} = 100^\circ$.
The angles $\angle HKJ$ and $\angle GKF$ are vertical angles.  Vertical angles are congruent.
The measure of $\angle HKJ$ is $100^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle centered at point O. Points F, G, H, and J all lie on the circle in a clockwise direction. Point K is in the circle to the right of the center of the circle, point O. A line segment connects point F to point G. A line segment connects point G to point J and goes through point K. A line segment connects point F to point H and goes through point K. Line segments F G, G K, and K F make triangle F G K. Angle H K J is made by line segment J K and line segment H K.

assessment_items.question_content:
On circle $O$ below, $\overline{ZX}$ is a diameter.  The measure of $\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{ZY}$ is $104^\circ$.

**What is the measure of $\angle OZY$?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$

>[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
The angle $\angle OXY$ is an inscribed angle that intercepts  $\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{ZY}$.

The measure of an inscribed angle is half the measure of the arc that it intercepts.

>$\green{\text{m} \angle OXY}=\dfrac{1}{2}(104^\circ)=\green{52^\circ}$

By the same reasoning, $\purple{\text{m} \angle ZYX}=\dfrac{1}{2}(180^\circ)=\purple{90^\circ}$

>[[☃ image 1]]

Points $X$, $Y$, and $Z$ form a triangle.

The measures of the interior angles of a triangle add up to $180^\circ$.  

>$\text{m}\angle OZY = 180^\circ - \green{90^\circ} - \purple{52^\circ} = 38^\circ$.
The measure of $\angle OZY$ is $38^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle centered at point O. Points X, Y, and Z all lie on the circle in a clockwise direction. Triangle X Y Z is inscribed in the circle with side X Z as a diameter for the circle.

assessment_items.question_content:
On circle $O$ below, $\overline{SE}$ is a diameter.  

**What is the measure of $\angle ISE$?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$

>[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
The angle $\angle SLE$ is an inscribed angle that intercepts  the diameter.

The measure of an inscribed angle is half the measure of the arc that it intercepts.

>$\green{\text{m} \angle SLE}=\dfrac{1}{2}(180^\circ)=\green{90^\circ}$

By the same reasoning, $\pink{\text{m} \angle SDE}=\dfrac{1}{2}(180^\circ)=\pink{90^\circ}$

Vertical angles are congruent, so $\blue{\text{m}\angle EID}=\text{m}\angle SIL$.

>[[☃ image 1]]

Points $I$, $D$, and $E$ form a triangle.

The measures of the interior angles of a triangle add up to $180^\circ$.  

>$\text{m}\angle IED = 180^\circ - \pink{90^\circ} - \blue{61^\circ} = 29^\circ$.

Points $S$, $D$, and $E$ also form a triangle.

The measure of $\angle SED$ is equal to the sums of measures of $\angle SEI$ and $\angle IED$.

>$27^\circ+29^\circ = 56^\circ$

The measures of the interior angles of a triangle add up to $180^\circ$.  

>$\begin{align}
\text{m}\angle ISE &= 180^\circ - \pink{90^\circ} - 56^\circ\\
& = 34^\circ\\
\end{align}$.
The measure of $\angle ISE$ is $34^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle centered at point O. Points A, L, D, and, E all lie on the circle in a clockwise direction. Triangles E L S and D E S both overlap and share a side S E. Side E L and side D S intersect at a point I. Angle L I S is sixty one degrees. Angle I E O is twenty-seven degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
On circle $O$ below, the measure of $\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{SV}$ is $120^\circ$. 

**What is the measure of $\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{VU}$?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$

>[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
The angle $\angle STU$ is an inscribed angle that intercepts  $\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{SU}$.

The measure of an inscribed angle is half the measure of the arc that it intercepts.

>$\text{m} \stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{SU}=2(\green{82^\circ})=164^\circ$

The arc measure of $\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{SU}$ is equal to the sum of the measures of $\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{SV}$ and $\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{VU}$.  So:

>$\text{m}\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{VU}=164^\circ-120^\circ$
The measure of $\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{VU}$ is $44^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle centered around point O with points S T U V all on the circle in a clockwise direction. Points S T U V form a quadrilateral where angle S T U is eighty-two degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
On circle $O$ below, the measure of $\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{LI}$ is $97^\circ$. 

**What is the measure of $\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{FL}$?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$

>[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
The opposite angles in ANY inscribed quadrilateral are supplementary.  [[☃ explanation 1]]

>$\text{m}\angle FPI = 180^\circ-110^\circ = 70^\circ$

The angle $\angle FPI$ is an inscribed angle that intercepts  $\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{FI}$.

The measure of an inscribed angle is half the measure of the arc that it intercepts.

>$\text{m} \stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{FI}=2(\green{70^\circ})=140^\circ$

The arc measure of $\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{FI}$ is equal to the sum of the measures of $\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{FL}$ and $\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{LI}$.  So:

>$\text{m}\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{FL}=140^\circ-97^\circ$
The measure of $\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{FL}$ is $43^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle centered around point O with points F L I P all on the circle in a clockwise direction. Points F L I P form a quadrilateral where angle F L I is one hundred ten degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the measure of $\angle DEG$ on circle $O$?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$

>[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
The angle $\green{\angle CFD}$ is an inscribed angle that intercepts  $\blue{\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{CD}}$.

The measure of an inscribed angle is half the measure of the arc that it intercepts.

>$\green{50^\circ} = \dfrac{1}{2}(\blue{\text{m}\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{CD}})$

So, $\blue{\text{m}\stackrel{\LARGE{\frown}}{CD}}=\blue{100^\circ}$.

The angles $\green{\angle CFD}$ and $\purple{\angle DEG}$ are inscribed angles that intercept the **same arc**.

By the same reasoning, $\purple{\text{m} \angle DEG}=\dfrac{1}{2}(\blue{100^\circ})=\purple{50^\circ}$.
The measure of $\angle DEG$ is $50^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle centered around point O where points C D E F all lie on the circle. Line segment C E forms a chord. Line segment D F forms a chord. These segments intersect at point G. Line segments D E and C F form chords. These form triangles C F G and D E G. Angle C F G is fifty degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
Quadrilateral $WILD$ is inscribed in circle $O$.   
$\overline{WI}$ is a diameter of circle $O$.

**What is the measure of $\angle D$?**  
[[☃ input-number 1]] $^\circ$


[[☃ image 1]]
hints: The opposite angles in ANY inscribed quadrilateral are supplementary.  [[☃ explanation 1]]
>$\pink{\text{m}\angle D} + \blue{45} = 180$
The measure of $\angle D$ is $135^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle centered around point O with points W I L D all on the circle in a clockwise direction. Points W I L D form a quadrilateral where side W I is a diameter, angle O I L is forty-five degrees, and angle I L D is one hundred nine degrees.

Quadrilateral $BEAR$ is inscribed in circle $O$.  

**What is the measure of $\angle R$?**

[[☃ image 1]]
hints: The opposite angles in ANY inscribed quadrilateral are supplementary.  [[☃ explanation 1]]
>$\pink{\text{m}\angle R} + \blue{74} = 180$
The measure of $\angle R$ is $106^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle centered around point O with points B E A R all on the circle in a clockwise direction. Point B E A R form a quadrilateral where angle B E A is seventy-four degrees and angle E A R is ninety-two degrees.
